Primary Objective: Minimize total operating cost across vendors, suppliers, delivery, and university support
Recommendation Logic: Recommend lower-cost menus, reduced production buffers, cost-efficient suppliers, and minimal subsidy use
Strengths: Improves cost control and may protect vendor margins under stable demand
Weaknesses: May reduce nutrition, satisfaction, redundancy, and resilience
Best-Fit Scenarios: Stable operation, low budget availability, inflation monitoring
Key Metrics to Monitor: Total cost, vendor profit, affordability, satisfaction, nutrition score, EVI
Risk / Trade-off: Overemphasis on cost can create hidden mission failure

Primary Objective: Maximize vendor and supplier financial viability
Recommendation Logic: Recommend production, pricing, and demand routing that increase sales and reduce unprofitable operations
Strengths: Improves business continuity and stakeholder willingness to participate
Weaknesses: May increase price and reduce affordability for students
Best-Fit Scenarios: Vendor exit risk, low sales periods, semester break
Key Metrics to Monitor: Vendor profit, supplier revenue, student affordability, demand fulfillment, EVI
Risk / Trade-off: Vendor viability may be improved at the expense of student welfare

Primary Objective: Maximize student satisfaction and meal access quality
Recommendation Logic: Recommend vendors, menus, order times, and delivery options that improve convenience, nutrition, and queue experience
Strengths: Improves user adoption, perceived service quality, and campus welfare
Weaknesses: May overload popular vendors or increase vendor cost
Best-Fit Scenarios: Lunch peak, exam week, high complaint periods
Key Metrics to Monitor: Satisfaction, waiting time, nutrition, affordability, vendor utilization, EVI
Risk / Trade-off: Optimizing student experience alone may reduce vendor sustainability

Primary Objective: Maximize nutritional quality and health-related mission achievement
Recommendation Logic: Recommend healthier menus, nutrition-aware student routing, and supplier choices that support better ingredients
Strengths: Aligns strongly with student welfare and institutional mission
Weaknesses: May increase cost and reduce short-term student acceptance if preferences differ
Best-Fit Scenarios: Nutrition policy, health campaign, welfare-oriented intervention
Key Metrics to Monitor: Nutrition score, satisfaction, affordability, vendor profit, mission achievement
Risk / Trade-off: Healthier options may be less affordable or less preferred without subsidy

Primary Objective: Minimize raw food waste, leftover meals, and environmental burden
Recommendation Logic: Recommend conservative production, better demand forecasting, menu substitution, and redistribution of surplus
Strengths: Improves sustainability and reduces unnecessary cost
Weaknesses: May increase shortage risk if demand is underestimated
Best-Fit Scenarios: Semester break, plastic ban, sustainability campaign, low-demand uncertainty
Key Metrics to Monitor: Waste reduction score, shortage, satisfaction, vendor profit, sustainability
Risk / Trade-off: Very low waste may mean insufficient buffer capacity

Primary Objective: Maximize student affordability and protect low-income student access
Recommendation Logic: Recommend low-cost menu options, targeted subsidy, price-sensitive vendor routing, and cost-sharing mechanisms
Strengths: Supports equity and student welfare during cost pressure
Weaknesses: May reduce vendor profit or require higher university subsidy
Best-Fit Scenarios: Inflation, scholarship meal expansion, cost shock
Key Metrics to Monitor: Affordability, subsidy cost, vendor profit, student satisfaction, EVI
Risk / Trade-off: Affordability gains may depend on limited subsidy budget

Primary Objective: Balance benefit distribution across students, vendors, and service locations
Recommendation Logic: Distribute demand and support so that no group is systematically disadvantaged
Strengths: Improves legitimacy, trust, and long-term participation
Weaknesses: May sacrifice short-term efficiency or profit maximization
Best-Fit Scenarios: New vendor entry, vendor exit, equity-sensitive subsidy policy
Key Metrics to Monitor: Fairness index, vendor profit variance, access equity, satisfaction, EVI
Risk / Trade-off: Fair distribution may reduce performance of the most efficient vendors

Primary Objective: Maximize ability to maintain service under disruption and recover quickly
Recommendation Logic: Maintain buffers, diversify supply, distribute demand, and activate backup capacity under stress
Strengths: Performs well under uncertainty, shocks, and compound disruptions
Weaknesses: May increase cost and reduce efficiency under normal conditions
Best-Fit Scenarios: Supply shock, vendor failure, power outage, pandemic, multi-shock crisis
Key Metrics to Monitor: Resilience, recovery time, shortage, EVI, sustainability, cost
Risk / Trade-off: Robustness requires redundancy, which may appear inefficient in normal operation

Primary Objective: Maximize environmental and social sustainability outcomes
Recommendation Logic: Recommend low-waste production, sustainable sourcing, low-emission delivery, and reusable packaging
Strengths: Aligns with institutional sustainability and regulatory objectives
Weaknesses: Can increase cost or require behavior change
Best-Fit Scenarios: Plastic ban, carbon tax, sustainability campaign, environmental reporting
Key Metrics to Monitor: Sustainability score, waste, emissions proxy, affordability, satisfaction
Risk / Trade-off: Sustainability improvements may require subsidy or stakeholder incentives

Primary Objective: Ensure safety, hygiene, fairness, and regulatory compliance before optimizing other objectives
Mathematical Objective Function: max Compliance(t), subject to Safety(t)≥θ_safety and Fairness(t)≥θ_fairness
Recommendation Logic: Filter or constrain all recommendations using safety, hygiene, labeling, and environmental rules
Strengths: Reduces institutional risk and protects public trust
Weaknesses: May constrain operational flexibility and reduce short-term performance
Best-Fit Scenarios: Food safety incident, regulatory audit, new compliance rule
Key Metrics to Monitor: Compliance rate, safety incidents, trust score, vendor viability, EVI
Risk / Trade-off: Strict compliance constraints can reduce menu diversity or vendor participation

Primary Objective: Maximize overall Ecosystem Value Index using weighted multi-objective optimization
Recommendation Logic: Balance fulfillment, mission achievement, sustainability, satisfaction, profitability, and waste reduction
Strengths: Provides balanced trade-off across stakeholders and mission dimensions
Weaknesses: Requires careful weight selection and may be less transparent than single-objective policies
Best-Fit Scenarios: General MOEE operation, policy comparison, normal-to-disrupted conditions
Key Metrics to Monitor: EVI, mission achievement, sustainability, satisfaction, profit, waste reduction
Risk / Trade-off: Poorly chosen weights can hide stakeholder-specific harm

Primary Objective: Select or blend MSRS policies dynamically according to scenario state and observed performance
Recommendation Logic: Switch between cost, nutrition, waste, resilience, fairness, or balanced policies based on scenario diagnosis
Strengths: Most flexible and potentially strongest under changing conditions
Weaknesses: Requires reliable scenario detection, learning, and governance oversight
Best-Fit Scenarios: Multi-shock crisis, long-term adaptive operation, uncertain environments
Key Metrics to Monitor: Policy switching accuracy, EVI, robustness, acceptance rate, prediction error
Risk / Trade-off: Adaptive behavior may be difficult to explain or audit
